channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Esports Nations Cup Releases Rocket League Rosters: Vatira, Atomic & Daniel Headline Names
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Foundation (formerly the Esports World Cup Foundation) has released the list of rosters set to compete in the Esports Nations Cup Rocket League tournament.

Big names show up for Esports Nations Cup Rocket League tournament

As expected, the Esports Nations Cup Rocket League tournament will feature some of the hardest-hitting players in the Rocket League space.

The list of names includes some of Rocket League’s most well-known and well-accomplished players, including two of NRG’s 2025 RLCS World Champions and four-time Major-winner Axel ‘Vatira’ Touret, fresh off of Karmine Corp’s record-breaking Paris Major triumph.

However, only two players from a single organization can play for their nation at the Esports Nations Cup, meaning some notable players are missing. As a result, Landon ‘BeastMode’ Konerman cannot join his NRG teammates on the United States’ roster.

Note: Each roster includes four players, the last of which acts as a substitute.

The top 10 rosters as per the Esports Nations Cup rankings are:

  1. France: Vatira, zen, juicy, ExoTiiK
  2. Saudi Arabia: Rw9, trk511, Kiileerrz, Nwpo
  3. United States: Atomic, Daniel, Firstkiller, Chronic
  4. South Africa: Snowyy, 2Die4, gunz, Sweaty
  5. Brazil: swiftt., ANXNZ, kv1, Sad
  6. Australia: gus, Fever, bananahead, hntr
  7. Spain: AtomiK, crr, stizzy, LuiisP
  8. Netherlands: Joreuz, oaly., nass, justuszzz
  9. Great Britain: Archie, Joyo, ApparentlyJack, Toxiic
  10. Mexico: kofyr, CHEESE., Pollo, S5-Cosmic

Below the top 10 teams, there is still plenty of talent, but that top talent does not have the same depth behind it as the top 10 rosters.

TSM’s Leonardo Raffael ‘Catalysm’ Christ Ramos will represent Germany, Vatira’s famous KC partner-in-crime Tristan ‘Atow.’ Soyez will represent Belgium, and SpaceStation Gaming’s Jack ‘reveal’ LeDuc will represent Canada.

The top 24 teams are directly invited to the event, with 21 more teams making the main event through qualification and three through the Esports Nations Cup Wildcard process.

The Esports Nations Cup Rocket League Regional Qualifiers will run from July 17-19 across North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and the Asia-Pacific regions.

To see the full list of Rocket League rosters for all nations, see here.

Do you think each nation made the right selection? Let us know on our Discord Server.
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All 007: First Light Cameos—Every Celebrity Cameo in 007: First Light
item.content:encoded:
Having a license to kill can be a lot of pressure for one man—James Bond. This is why IO Interactive backs up England’s secret-service stalwart with a plethora of cameos in 007: First Light.

There were question marks about whether IO Interactive could make 007: First Light different enough from Hitman. All doubts have been dispelled as 007: First Light is the highest-rated James Bond game in 30 years.

007: First Light is a masterful, must-own title. If you do buy it, you might want to see what cameos await you!

Every 007: First Light Cameo

Chase and Status

As you can see above, England-based Chase & Status were confirmed for 007: First Light ahead of time. They feature in a very Hitman-esque setting mission where Bond visits an underground club.

  • More Insider Gaming: How Many Chapters in 007: First Light? Complete Mission List

The music you can hear pumping is from them, and James could be forgiven for getting his rave on with a Vodka Martini (shaken, not stirred).

Khaby Lame

Worldwide TikTok star Khaby Lame is in 007: First Light—what an age we live in.

The most-followed TikTok personality in the world has a brief cameo in 007: First Light. It’s completely on brand as it’s a voiceless appearance, but captures Lame’s trademark shtick that millions of people love.

Jacksepticeye

Is there anyone who doesn’t like Jacksepticeye? The iconic YouTuber has extended their resume beyond the confines of uploads and Let’s Plays by delving into acting and voice acting.

Sean has recently had a role in Dispatch, Bendy and the Dark Revival, Poppy Playtime, and now 007: First Light.

Shroud

Michael Grzesiek, AKA Shroud, is up next. With nearly seven million subscribers on YouTube and constantly one of the most-followed Twitch streamers in history, Shroud is a big name in gaming.

The former professional Counter-Strike player and Valorant pro has scored himself a cheeky cameo in 007: First Light.

Unconfirmed & Fake Cameos in 007: First Light

I debated about doing this section because any number of fakes can appear on the internet in 2026, but there are a few notable examples of alleged 007: First Light cameos I’ve seen online, and I thought I’d point them out, just so you know.

Ricky Berwick

Now, Ricky Berwick, appearing in 007: First Light, would be tremendous. However, I think this is one we can definitely file under photoshopped or AI.

xQc

With other famous streamers and content creators already in the game, it wouldn’t be a shock to see xQc in First Light. I think it’s another case of, shall we say, mistaken identity, or a faked image.

  • More Insider Gaming: Complete 007 First Light Trophy List

Especially as xQc hasn’t mentioned or referenced being in the game.

If, for whatever reason, any of these are wrong, I will update the list. On the other hand, if any other notable cameos have been missed, please let me know by messaging me (or the team) through the Insider Gaming Discord!

Also, check out the first-ever Insider Gaming Showcase live on May 28, featuring 55 games and 12 World Premieres.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Valorant 12.10 Patch Brings Masters London Pick’Ems, Skirmish Maps, and Replay Sharing
item.content:encoded:
Valorant has released its latest 12.10 patch, adding the Valorant Masters London Pick’Ems, new Skirmish maps, and Replay Friend Sharing to the tactical FPS.

The patch will act as the final patch before Valorant Masters London begins, but it does not touch the meta in what has already been a disrupted period for Valorant’s agent pool.

The 12.09 patch saw nerfs to Neon and Shotgun movement accuracy as urgent fixes before nMasters London begins, but the VCT will have to wait for the meta-defining changes to Sentinels and Initiators until after Masters London, when the 13.00 patch drops.

Replay Friend Sharing introduced

The most esports-adjacent patch introduces Replay Friend Sharing, which will allow players to share replays of their games in Competitive, Unrated, Swiftplay, Premier, and Custom modes.

Riot notes that “Replays for Custom games are only available when the lobby owner enables recording in the Custom options menu before the game starts. Recording must be enabled again for each new game.”

The replays should be a helpful tool for players looking to improve parts of their play, allowing teammates and friends to watch their games and see where to improve.

Valorant Masters London Pick’Ems added

Masters London Pick’Ems have been added to the game, with the Swiss Stage selections now available. Players will get points for correctly predicting who will advance from the eight-team Swiss stage, and get extra points when they correctly predict the 2-0 teams.

Players will get an exclusive title for playing the Pick’Ems and earn additional prizes depending on how well they do throughout the tournament.

To see who we think will do well, check out our Valorant Masters London Tournament Guide!

Two skirmish maps added

Riot also added Skimish Maps D and E to the Skirmish mode, with different configurations of boxes, ramps, and covers to help players test their aim.

Additionally, all Agents will be available for Skirmish: Ascension regardless of whether players have unlocked that Agent. Seems like a good way to test them, before you use up your resources to unlock an Agent.

Valorant 12.09 Patch Bug Fixes

The devs also noted a list of bug fixes, directly shared from patch notes below:

  • Agents
    • Miks
      • Fixed a bug where Miks’s M-Pulse healing would persist briefly on enemies after they exit its range.
      • Fixed a bug where Miks’s M-Pulse could cancel other ability and weapon equip animations
      • Fixed a bug where spamming alt-fire with Miks’s M-Pulse could cause it to be used in the wrong form.
    • Harbor
      • Fixed a bug where Harbor’s Reckoning could disappear early if the player spammed the cast button
  • Gameplay
    • Fixed a bug that allowed players to inadvertently kill enemies from spawn during the buy phase. This fix was rolled out with a patch redeploy during 12.09.
  • Modes
    • Skirmish 2v2: Fixed a bug where the timer was stuck at 0.5 seconds in overtime
  • Store
    • Fixed an issue where bundle details were not displayed on the confirmation page when purchasing VCT bundles.

CONSOLE ONLY

  • Store
    • Fixed a clipping issue when confirming a gun buddy purchase from the battlepass.

Would you have liked to see more meta changes before the Valorant Masters London? Let us know on our Discord Server.
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Upcoming Game Pass Titles Surface Ahead Of XBOX Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Eagle-eyed fans have noticed that some updates have been made to the Coming Soon section of XBOX Game Pass, as a host of new games have appeared, including the likes of Clockwork Revolution and Fable.

Given the timing of these additions, many believe each of these games could be featured at the upcoming XBOX Games Showcase on June 7, 2026, with release dates a real possibility.

Have Some XBOX Games Showcase Titles Leaked?

New titles are constantly being added to the XBOX Game Pass Coming Soon section, but the latest batch has fans particularly excited, and for good reason. First noticed by Reddit user Weird-Judge-4578, games featured in the category at least have a release window attached, but this time, several titles with completely TBA launch dates have suddenly appeared, including:

  • Clockwork Revolution
  • Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy
  • Tropico 7
  • Fable
  • There Are No Ghosts At The Grand
  • Armatus
  • Witchbrook

It’s an eye-catching line-up filled with highly anticipated releases. Fable is naturally one of the biggest names on the list, and it was even recently rated in Korea, while Clockwork Revolution has already built up a passionate fanbase eager to see more. Even Witchbrook, which has famously been in development for over a decade, has now surfaced in the Coming Soon section.

The timing has also raised plenty of eyebrows. With a major XBOX showcase just around the corner, where release date announcements are widely expected, many fans believe it can’t be a coincidence that so many TBA titles were added at once.

Whether that means all seven games will appear during the event remains to be seen, so it’s probably best to keep expectations in check for now. At the very least, the additions confirm that each title will launch day one on XBOX Game Pass, which is a huge win for subscribers. And who knows, by the time the showcase wraps up on June 7, we may finally have release dates for all of them.

What else are you hoping to see at the XBOX Games Showcase? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server, and don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For more gaming news, read about the official announcement for The Witcher 3’s upcoming expansion, as well as the malware concern caused by GTA 6 pre-orders.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Pokémon Pokopia Bundle to Release in Europe
item.content:encoded:
A new Nintendo Switch 2 bundle is set to release in Europe, featuring a copy of the hit life-simulation title Pokémon Pokopia, it has been revealed.

Pokémon Pokopia was released to rave reviews earlier this year and is the first Pokémon title to release exclusively on the Switch 2, with Pokémon Winds & Waves set to follow.

Price rises to Nintendo Switch 2 hardware, and other consoles, have hit the industry recently, but those in Europe looking to save some money picking up the console alongside Pokémon Pokopia are set to have another option.

Switch 2 Bundle with Pokémon Pokopia Set to Release

Following on from confirmed release periods in the Americas, Australia, and New Zealand, all of which will be available from early June, Dealabs has revealed that a Switch 2 bundle coming alongside Pokémon Pokopia is also on the horizon.

Similar to other regions, the bundle is expected to be available in June, but there will be some packaging differences, as it’s stated the left-side of the box will feature a Ditto, the Pokémon that players take control of in Pokopia.

Additionally, it’s revealed the bundle will have a suggested retail price of €509, but this will only remain in effect until September 1, 2026, when price rises for the console will come into effect.

However, retailers are expected to offer discounted rates, which could reduce the price closer to €455. Again, though, expect the prices to be raised later this year.

Bundling Pokopia with a Switch 2 console makes a lot of sense, considering the staggering sales numbers and scores from critics and players alike it has received, and it could be one of the most popular items to purchase in the holiday season later this year.

Fans are eagerly awaiting to hear any news of additional content coming to Pokopia, with a growing hope for DLC, especially given that the tenth generation of the franchise won’t be released until 2027.

Is the Switch 2 Pokopia bundle a good deal? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP
